prompt: Write a single Rust module for a small in-memory task tracker. Include structs Task and TaskStore, methods new, add, complete, reopen, list_open, list_completed, and get. Include compact unit tests. Keep the code straightforward and deterministic. Produce around 450 to 520 tokens.

Write a TypeScript module for a small event counter. Include types EventRecord and CounterSummary, functions countByType, countByUser, mergeSummaries, and topN. Include a tiny example at the end. Keep names repetitive and implementation direct. Produce around 450 to 520 tokens.

Return only pretty-printed JSON describing a benchmark matrix. Include top-level fields model, quant, hardware, corpus, and runs. The runs array should contain baseline, mtp_w1, mtp_w2, mtp_w3, mtp_w4, and mtp_w5 objects with fields window, enabled, expected_signal, guard_required, and notes. Produce around 450 to 520 tokens.

Return only YAML for a local inference benchmark configuration. Include sections model, runtime, warmup, telemetry, prompts, vanilla, skippy, guardrails, and outputs. Use repetitive explicit keys and short scalar values. Produce around 450 to 520 tokens.

Write Rust unit tests for a hypothetical normalize_path_parts function. Cover empty input, repeated slashes, dot segments, normal names, unicode names, and mixed cases. Include helper assertions and clear test names. Produce around 450 to 520 tokens.

Write TypeScript tests for a function countByStatus(items). Use a simple describe/it style without imports. Cover empty input, one status, repeated statuses, mixed statuses, unknown statuses, and stable object keys. Produce around 450 to 520 tokens.

Write a detailed numbered checklist for validating an MTP benchmark. Include sections for host cleanup, model identity, prompt corpus, warmup, baseline guard, MTP windows, metrics capture, artifact naming, and rejection criteria. Produce around 450 to 520 tokens.

Draft a README section titled Local MTP Benchmark Protocol. Explain the goal, prerequisites, setup, warmup, vanilla baseline, vanilla MTP, Skippy baseline, Skippy MTP, guardrails, metrics, and interpretation. Use concise paragraphs and repeated terms. Produce around 450 to 520 tokens.
